v0.1.0 MIT License 119 tests passing GitHub PyPI

llm-eval-psychometrics

Psychometric diagnostics for large language model benchmarks.

A Python toolkit that brings Item Response Theory and multidimensional demand profiling to LLM evaluation. Validated on MMLU-Pro across ten open-source models, with findings that interrogate what benchmarks actually measure.

Contents
  1. Overview
  2. Item Response Theory
  3. Demand Profiling
  4. Key Findings
  5. Quick Start
  6. Modules
§ I · Overview

What this toolkit does.

LLM benchmarks behave like psychological tests: a set of items administered to a population of subjects, scored, and aggregated into a summary number. Psychometrics has spent a century learning to interrogate whether such summaries mean what we think they mean. This project brings that machinery to benchmark evaluation.

Item Response Theory

Fit 2PL models to binary response matrices. Estimate item difficulty and discrimination. Generate interpretable ICC, IIC, TIC, and Wright Map diagnostics that reveal which items actually measure anything.

Bias and reliability detection

Compute Cronbach's alpha, flag low-discrimination items, and identify benchmark items that fail to differentiate between strong and weak models — the ones every LLM gets right, or gets right for the wrong reasons.

Multidimensional demand profiling

Decompose benchmark performance into eighteen cognitive dimensions inspired by Zhou et al. (2026, Nature), enabling instance-level prediction of where a given model will succeed and where it will fail.

§ II · Item Response Theory

Measuring what the benchmark measures.

IRT models each item as a function of a latent ability parameter, producing per-item and per-model diagnostics that accuracy scores cannot. The four plots below are generated from a 2PL fit on MMLU-Pro responses from ten open-source LLMs.

§ III · Demand Profiling · New

From one number to eighteen.

Inspired by Zhou et al. (2026, Nature), this module decomposes each benchmark item into a vector of eighteen cognitive demands — logical reasoning, numerical computation, linguistic comprehension, domain knowledge, and others — and combines those demands with model-specific ability profiles to predict instance-level outcomes.

Key idea Instead of reducing an item to a single difficulty parameter, each item becomes a profile — a vector across eighteen cognitive capabilities. Combined with each model's ability profile, this enables prediction of whether a specific model will succeed on a specific item, not just average performance.
§ IV · Key Findings

What we learned from MMLU-Pro.

10LLMs
Open-source models analyzed
45%
Items failing to discriminate
18dims
Cognitive capability axes
n = 1
Instance-level prediction

Finding. Nearly half of MMLU-Pro items exhibit low discrimination (a < 0.5), meaning they fail to distinguish between high- and low-ability models. Demand profiling reveals that these items tend to load heavily on surface-level knowledge retrieval rather than deeper reasoning dimensions — a structural property of the benchmark that simple accuracy scores cannot expose.

§ V · Quick Start

Install and fit your first model.

listing 1 python 
# Install from PyPI
pip install llm-eval-psychometrics

import numpy as np
from llm_eval_psychometrics import irt

# Binary response matrix: rows = models, columns = items
responses = np.loadtxt("response_matrix.csv", delimiter=",")

# Fit a 2PL IRT model
model = irt.fit_2pl(responses)

# Generate diagnostic plots
irt.plot_icc(model)
irt.plot_wright_map(model)
irt.plot_tic(model)
listing 2 python 
from llm_eval_psychometrics import demand

# Load items with demand annotations
profiles  = demand.load_profiles("items.json")

# Estimate each model's ability profile across 18 dimensions
abilities = demand.estimate_abilities(responses, profiles)

# Predict and validate instance-level outcomes
preds = demand.predict(abilities, profiles)
demand.plot_radar(profiles)
demand.plot_validity(preds, responses)
§ VI · Modules

Package structure.

ModulePurposeKey functions
irt 2PL item response theory: parameter estimation, item and test information, Wright maps. fit_2pl · plot_icc
bias Differential item functioning analysis to detect items that behave differently across model families. detect_dif · flag_biased
reliability Internal consistency metrics including Cronbach's alpha, split-half reliability, and item-total correlations. cronbach_alpha · split_half
demand new Cognitive demand profiling across eighteen dimensions with instance-level prediction. load_profiles · predict

llm-eval-psychometrics — Built and maintained by Ruoyi Zhu. Inspired by a century of psychometric theory and the recent work of Zhou et al. (2026) in Nature. Released under the MIT license.
